Ad Text:
The Mission: As our Performance Marketing Manager, you will be the strategic lead for NLA’s digital media growth engine. We are looking for a data-first marketer to elevate our performance capability, moving beyond standard metrics to build sophisticated, high-value acquisition models. You will own the digital roadmap, leveraging deep data insights, best-in-class performance creative, and cutting-edge experimentation to ensure we remain the definitive 'Pace Setters' in the EV and leasing market.

Key Responsibilities:

Strategic Channel Mastery: Drive the end-to-end strategy across Paid Search, Paid Social, and emerging channels. You will be responsible for the health and scaling of our digital presence, ensuring we are always at the forefront of the market.

Advanced Meta Optimisation: Lead our strategy on Meta with a focus on algorithm-led growth. You will be responsible for leveraging Advantage+ campaigns, optimising for high-intent signals, and ensuring our structure is built to scale efficiently in a privacy-first environment.

Performance-Led Creative Strategy: Partner with our design team to deliver best-in-class performance creative. You will move beyond "static" ads to lead a "hook-and-hold" approach—analysing creative retention rates and using data to brief the next iteration of high-performing assets.

Ad Format & Placement Experimentation: Own a rigorous testing roadmap across all formats (Reels, Carousels, Collection Ads, and YouTube Shorts). You will constantly push the boundaries of how we show up, testing new formats against traditional benchmarks to uncover the most effective way to engage our audience.

Google Product Innovation: Act as the internal expert on Google’s evolving product suite. You will lead experimentation with PMax, AI-driven bidding strategies, and beta features to ensure NLA maintains a technical advantage over the competition.

Leveraging First-Party Data: Work at the intersection of marketing and data science. You will lead the way in using our rich internal data and EV ownership insights to build highly targeted, intelligent audiences within our CRM and ad platforms.

Advanced Commercial & ROI Modelling: Move beyond basic conversion tracking to develop sophisticated ROI models. You will focus on the full customer lifecycle, ensuring our media spend is optimised for long-term business value and commercial efficiency.

What we’re looking for:

The Analytical Edge: You have a background in performance marketing where data, not just intuition, drives every decision. You are comfortable with complex attribution models and ROI forecasting.

The Creative Eye: You understand that in modern performance marketing, "creative is the new targeting." You know how to read a creative report to see where a video is losing its audience and how to fix it.

Technical Expertise: Deep, hands-on experience with Meta Ads Manager and Google Ads. You should be an expert in signal-based marketing and automated bidding strategies.

Data Savvy: Experience working with CRM data (ideally HubSpot) to power performance campaigns. You understand the value of first-party data and how to use it to fuel platform algorithms.

Commercial Curiosity: You don’t just look at clicks; you want to understand the bottom-line impact. You are comfortable discussing margin, customer lifetime value, and commercial efficiency.

Success Criteria (Experience & Qualifications)

Experience: 6–8+ years in performance marketing, with a proven track record of managing high monthly budgets.

Platform Expertise: Certified mastery of Google Ads and Meta Ads Manager is essential.

Data Literacy: Deep experience in ROI/ROAS modelling and using first-party data (CRM/HubSpot) to fuel ad platform algorithms.

Education: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Mathematics, or a related analytical field.

Industry Background: Experience in high-value lead generation, automotive, or marketplace industries is highly regarded.

Take hold of a monster opportunity and join our Transformation team as an Operating Model Design Specialist.  If you’re a good fit for this role and live in Brisbane, Sydney or Melbourne, we’d love you to apply.

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. As the Operating Model Design Specialist, you’ll lead the design and implementation of enterprise level operating models that support ART’s strategic priorities, regulatory obligations, and performance outcomes.


This role will play a key role in shaping how our enterprise operates — aligning strategy, structure, governance and capability to enable delivery at scale.


This is a high impact role for someone who operates confidently across strategy, design and delivery, and enjoys partnering with senior leaders to translate strategic intent into practical, sustainable operating models. You’ll shape how work is organised and delivered across the enterprise, creating clarity, accountability and alignment.

 

Day to day, you'll:

Design current‑state and future‑state operating models aligned to enterprise strategy.
Partner with executive and senior leaders to realign teams, capabilities and ways of working.
Simplify and align governance and decision‑making frameworks.
Define and embedd clear performance outcomes linked to strategic priorities.
Facilitate workshops and co‑design sessions with business leaders.
Develop and maintain enterprise artefacts such as capability maps, org structures and process frameworks.
Provide thought leadership on operating model design and enterprise transformation.
Collaborate with Change, People & Culture and Strategy teams to support effective implementation.
Monitor the effectiveness of operating models and drive continuous improvement.
Ensure alignment with risk, compliance and regulatory requirements.

This role is a permanent opportunity.

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

Proven experience in operating model design, organisational design or enterprise transformation.
Strong strategic thinking with the ability to translate strategy into practical design outcomes.
Confidence engaging and influencing senior and executive stakeholders.
Experience leading complex, cross‑functional initiatives.
Excellent facilitation, communication and presentation skills.
Knowledge of governance, performance management and capability frameworks.
An adaptive leadership style — comfortable navigating change and ambiguity.


About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

Apply online now

Everyone is welcome to apply. We value diverse thinking, cultures, perspectives, backgrounds, and abilities. Awaken your career at Australian Retirement Trust and apply now. Applications close Thursday, 26 March 2026.

We don’t want to leave you with a monstrous wait. So, you’ll hear from us after the closing date to find out whether you’ve made it through to the next round or not. Being fair and up-front is important to us – and recruitment is no different.

Location: Berkeley Vale, Gosford & Central Coast NSW (Hybrid)
Ad Text:
Who are we?

Sanitarium Health Food Company is a progressive and dynamic business that is Australian owned and produces Australia’s leading breakfast products – Up & Go, So Good, Alternative Dairy Co range and Australia’s favourite breakfast Weet-Bix.




The Role
Reporting to the Head of Category – AU & NZ, the Senior Category Development Manager will play a key role in shaping and executing category strategies that drive growth for Sanitarium and our retail partners. You’ll lead with insight, innovation, and collaboration across internal teams and external stakeholders.




Key Responsibilities:

Champion global best practices in category vision, category strategy development, range and layout management.
Build and maintain strong relationships with key trade partners to co-create strategies that deliver mutual growth.
Collaborate closely with the Senior Insights Manager to translate consumer and shopper data into actionable insights.
Align category thinking across Sales and Marketing to enhance integrated business planning and execution.
Contribute to annual business planning, brand strategy, and key commercial initiatives.
Provide strategic direction across all point-of-purchase elements including pricing, promotion, distribution, and layout.




What you’ll bring:

Tertiary qualifications in a relevant business field.
10+ years’ experience in category development, Sales, Marketing or related roles.
Strong people leadership, influencing, and stakeholder engagement skills.
A passion for delivering results and driving customer satisfaction.




What we offer

Half-day Fridays
Access to a range of workplace health and wellbeing initiatives
Salary sacrifice options
Achievement awards and recognition programs
Continuous learning and development opportunities
Study assistance to support role requirements
Staff store and canteen
Opportunity to contribute to community initiatives




All applicants must be able to demonstrate that they have the legal right to work in Australia.




We are an organisation driven by our mission and values. If you share our passion for what we do, our products and can respect our principles then this is a great opportunity for you. 




Hear why our employee’s love working at Sanitarium Health and Wellbeing, where people and purpose matter.  https://www.sanitarium.com.au/about/join-our-team/working-at-sanitarium

Perth's longest-standing private radiology practice since 1948

3 days per week, flexible hours, hybrid working

Senior data role with genuine scope and autonomy

About us

Established in 1948, we are Perth's longest-standing private radiology and medical imaging practice — and one of the largest in Australia. With over 20 clinics across Perth's hospitals and community sites, 130+ doctors, and more than 1,250 healthcare professionals and support staff, we're a diagnostic imaging leader known for our innovative approach and commitment to patient care.

Our people are the engine behind that reputation, and our investment in technology and data capability is a key part of how we're building for the future.

The opportunity

This is a newly defined, senior individual contributor role sitting at the intersection of data engineering, governance, and analytics. You'll lead PRC's data strategy — setting direction, driving maturity, and getting your hands dirty with the work that matters.

You'll own the organisation's approach to data quality, governance, and platform development, working closely with cyber security, business stakeholders, and technical teams to ensure data across PRC is trusted, accessible, and fit for purpose. With two direct reports, you'll provide hands-on technical leadership while operating as the primary data authority for the business.

This role is offered on a part-time basis — three days per week — with genuine flexibility around how those hours are structured. It's a hybrid arrangement, blending office-based collaboration with the option to work from home. This is a 12-month fixed-term position, covering parental leave.

What you'll be doing

In this role you'll set and drive PRC's data strategy, ensuring data is high quality, well governed, and accessible across the organisation. You'll lead hands-on delivery of complex data requests — including modelling, transformation, cleansing, and analysis — while developing and maintaining the governance frameworks that keep data consistent, reliable, and traceable.

You'll oversee data marts, models, and reference data to support operational and analytical reporting, and partner with business departments to embed data stewardship and define enterprise standards. A key part of the role is driving platform maturity across ETL/ELT pipelines, OLAP structures, and BI tooling, alongside leading and mentoring two direct reports in the data engineering and information management team.

You'll also coordinate responses to data-related incidents, conducting root-cause analysis and implementing improvements, and build data literacy across the organisation through targeted training and capability-building. Underpinning all of this is a commitment to compliance with relevant data regulations, privacy requirements, and internal policies.

What we're looking for

You're a technically strong data professional who can lead without losing touch with the work. You're comfortable owning a function, influencing stakeholders, and rolling up your sleeves — and you bring a track record of building data capability in complex organisations.

You'll hold a relevant tertiary qualification in IT, Computer Science, Data Science, or a related discipline, and bring strong SQL skills across extraction, transformation, cleansing, and analysis. You'll have hands-on experience with data platforms and tools including ETL/ELT frameworks, OLAP cubes, and enterprise BI platforms such as Cognos, along with proficiency in Power BI, Fabric, or similar analytics and visualisation tools — including data modelling and semantic layer design.

You'll have demonstrated experience developing, implementing, and maturing data governance frameworks, and proven leadership capability — meaning you've built and guided technical teams, not just managed tasks. Strong communication skills are essential, particularly the ability to translate complex data concepts clearly for non-technical audiences.

Rounding this out, you'll bring knowledge of data incident response, quality remediation, and root-cause analysis processes, along with a continuous learning mindset and genuine interest in where data management and analytics technologies are heading.

What we offer

Financial

Competitive, benchmarked remuneration with regular salary reviews

Discounted PRC services for team members and family

Comprehensive retail, lifestyle, and gym benefits

Novated leasing options

Leave and flexibility

15 weeks paid parental leave

Flexible working arrangements, where applicable

Option to purchase additional leave

Long service leave after 7 years

Workplace perks

Parking subsidies available

Uniforms provided

Wellbeing and support

Confidential Employee Assistance Program for you and your family

Recognition and culture

Annual Excellence Awards

Peer recognition through our Thank a Legend program

Weekly CEO newsletter and transparent internal communications

Annual all-staff Christmas party and regular team events

Interested?

We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, individuals with disabilities, and people from diverse cultural and social backgrounds. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

To register your interest, please apply now.

Salary: Competitive base salary + multiple bonus schemes
Ad Text:
Monday to Friday - Perth airport location and moving to West Perth
Performance, Retention, and Profit Share Bonuses
Company contribution towards private health insurance
Two-Year Maximum Term Contract
Join a high-performing in-house team of passionate risk and assurance professionals

About the role

Our Risk and Compliance team is seeking a Senior Advisor Risk and Assurance to join us in Perth. Reporting to the Manager Risk and Assurance, you will play a key role in strengthening our enterprise risk management framework across Hancock Iron Ore.

This role provides the opportunity to influence decision-making at every level of the organisation. You will be central to developing, maintaining and verifying high‑quality risk and control information, ensuring our leaders are equipped with accurate insights for confident strategic and operational decisions.

You will also help uplift organisational capability by delivering targeted coaching and training, while supporting governance, audit and assurance activities. This is a role for someone who enjoys variety, meaningful responsibility, and the chance to interface with senior leaders and operational teams alike.

Duties

Facilitate risk workshops, reviews and assessments aligned to the Risk Management Plan or business requirements
Support governance committee activities, including coordination, data preparation and reporting
Conduct regular site visits and build strong relationships with site-based leaders and teams
Contribute to the continuous improvement of systems, policies, procedures and governance processes
Partner with stakeholders across Roy Hill and the broader Hancock group to drive consistent, high‑quality risk practices

Skills and experience

Degree qualifications in Commerce, Engineering or related disciplines
Demonstrated experience in a risk-focused role
Experience in Business Continuity Planning, Disaster Recovery or Crisis Management
Knowledge of enterprise risk management frameworks, methodologies and tools
Strong facilitation skills, particularly in conducting workshops
Exceptional communication, influencing and engagement ability, with a natural capacity to build trust and credibility

About North East Link 

The Spark Consortium has been selected to deliver the Central Package of the North East Link Project - Victoria's biggest road project.

The Spark Consortium’s Design and Construction Contractor - which comprises Webuild, CPB Contractors, GS Engineering and Construction and China Construction Oceania - will deliver the twin three-lane tunnels and key interchanges for the project.

The Central Package will be delivered as a Public Private Partnership and provides three-lane twin tunnels that will close the missing link in Melbourne’s freeway network from the M80 Ring Road to an upgraded Eastern Freeway, reducing journey times and diverting heavy vehicles away from local roads. Up to 135,000 vehicles will use North East Link every day, reducing congestion in the north-east while maintaining local roads.

About the Opportunity

To support the delivery of the North East Link Project, we are seeking an experienced Commissioning Engineer to support the Mechanical, Electrical and Intelligent Transport Systems (MEI) program through high-quality data, reporting and process standardisation.

Joining our established MEI team, your primary focus will be to build, maintain critical reports and dashboards that keep our multi-billion-dollar MEI delivery on track. You will transform raw data from our core systems into clear, actionable information that drives daily decision-making across construction, commissioning and asset handover

While the emphasis is on MEI testing and commissioning workflows, the role is open to applicants from any background who bring strong analytical, process and data standardisation capabilities. If you have experience standardising ways of working, and translating complex delivery challenges into actionable solutions, we welcome your application, regardless of your prior exposure to MEI-specific systems.

This role will be accountable for:

Develop and maintain critical MEI reports and dashboards
Ensure data is consistent and reliable across multiple systems
Document MEI related workflows and standardise ways of working (process and data)
Develop and test hypothesis
Understand and support the maintenance of MEI user journey maps
Support Project Handover and Spark MEI stakeholders to develop workflow requirements and iterative changes during remaining project phases. 
Ongoing collaboration with stakeholders and execution of change management activities

Minimum requirements for this role:

3–7 years’ experience in reporting, delivery support or business improvement roles with a strong data and performance focus.
Degree qualification in Business, IT, Data Analytics or a related discipline. Project management credentials are highly regarded.
Strong capability in Power BI (or equivalent), advanced Excel (Power Query, complex formulas, pivot tables) and SQL.
Hands-on experience using tools such as Jira, Visio and/or Figma to document workflows and manage requirements.
Experience in construction, engineering or major infrastructure projects is desirable but not essential. Candidates from other sectors with strong reporting capability are encouraged to apply.
 Exposure to requirements gathering and process mapping is advantageous

What we Offer:

Ongoing career support and development opportunities
Generous parental leave
Strong culture charter
Quarterly social events
Reward and recognition program
Flexible working arrangements
Competitive salary

If this opportunity is of interest to you, please click on the ‘Apply now’ button to register your interest and please also subscribe to ‘Job Alerts’ to be notified of upcoming opportunities with SPARK.

Whilst we are keen to hear from candidates with proven infrastructure project experience or experience working for a Tier 1 or Tier 2 Contractor to be a part of an iconic construction project in Melbourne, Victoria, we also will consider applicants who are seeking to enter or re-enter the industry.

SPARK is committed to equal employment opportunity (EEO) and actively seeks to remove barriers people may face in gaining a successful position on the North East Link Central Package. We value diversity, inclusion and equity through providing a working environment where all employees feel included, valued, and free to bring their different skills, experiences and perspectives.

Deliver High-Impact ERP Solutions in a Growing Microsoft Practice

Work Across Multiple Projects and Mentor Junior Team Members Adelaide-Based

Competitive salary packages dependant on candidate experience




The Opportunity

Ever dreamed of working where your analytical brain, business know-how and technical flair are not just valued but celebrated? Welcome to the role that lets you lead with logic and intuition.

Are you an experienced ERP consultant looking to work on meaningful projects and mentor the next generation of functional talent? Join Solutions+, a leading Australian and New Zealand ERP solutions provider, and help deliver smart, scalable solutions to a wide range of clients.

As a Senior Functional Consultant, you’ll manage multiple ERP implementation projects, contribute to solution design, and oversee junior consultants within project teams. This is a dynamic role with scope to grow into a senior leadership position.




Key Outcomes of the Role

Deliver ERP projects aligned to project schedules and quality standards

Lead and participate in workshops, requirement gathering and solution design

Support multiple projects concurrently with minimal supervision

Peer-review small projects and support junior consultants with complex tickets

Manage support tickets and ensure customer satisfaction through clear communication


About You




To succeed in this role, you will bring:

5+ years of experience implementing Microsoft Business Central (or similar ERP)

Strong industry knowledge

Strong understanding of project delivery methodologies and business processes

Experience in customer-facing consulting roles

Ability to lead workshops, document requirements and contribute to solution design

A passion for mentoring junior staff and sharing your expertise

Strong organisational and communication skills

About Our Client

Solutions+ is a respected ERP consultancy headquartered in Adelaide. Their Microsoft Business Unit is expanding, supporting a growing portfolio of clients across multiple industries. With a strong culture of learning, collaboration, and innovation, Solutions+ offers the ideal environment for functional consultants ready to elevate their impact.

If you're looking for the next step in your ERP consulting career, this is it.




We've shared the client's name to help you assess your suitability for the role.  Please DO NOT contact them directly.




Apply Now

If you have any queries, please email humans(at)humanologyrecruit.com.au or call 1300 2 HUMAN.

Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
Join a respected organisation shaping the healthcare education sector.
Lead Agile delivery of impactful digital and transformation initiatives.
Flexible work from home arrangement (3 day's in the office)

About the role:

Our client is a respected professional membership organisation that supports education, training, and standards across the healthcare sector in Australia and New Zealand. As part of their ongoing digital transformation, they're looking for an experienced Agile Delivery Lead to help guide the delivery of key technology initiatives.

You'll work closely with delivery teams, product owners, and business stakeholders to keep projects on track, remove blockers, and embed strong Agile practices. It's a role suited to someone who enjoys bringing teams together and helping them deliver real outcomes.

Key Responsibilities:


Lead the end-to-end delivery of digital and business initiatives using Agile frameworks such as Scrum, Kanban, SAFe, or hybrid delivery models.
Establish and embed Agile ways of working across delivery squads, promoting continuous improvement and iterative value delivery.
Facilitate Agile ceremonies including sprint planning, reviews, retrospectives, and program increment planning where applicable.
Ensure alignment between product road maps, strategic objectives, and delivery execution.
Partner with Product Owners, clinical leaders, education stakeholders, and business units to clarify priorities and outcomes.
Provide transparent communication and reporting on delivery progress, risks, and outcomes to stakeholders including senior leadership.
Manage RAID (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies) and ensure delivery aligns with governance, compliance, and reporting frameworks.
Lead and coach multidisciplinary teams, including business analysts, developers, vendors, and subject matter experts.
Coordinate and manage third-party vendors and implementation partners to ensure contractual and delivery outcomes are achieved.
Monitor delivery metrics such as velocity, cycle time, burn-down, and predictability, using insights to drive improvements in processes and tooling.

Essential Experience:


5+ year's experience in delivery leadership roles such as Delivery Lead, Program Manager, or Senior Project Manager.
Demonstrated experience delivering digital or transformation initiatives in Agile environments.
Strong understanding and practical experience with Agile frameworks (Scrum, Kanban, SAFe or similar).
Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams and coordinate complex delivery programs.
Experience working within governance-heavy or regulated environments, ensuring compliance and risk management requirements are met.
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills, including the ability to work effectively with executive and technical audiences.
Strong capabilities in risk management, dependency management, and delivery planning.
Ability to influence without authority and remove impediments to enable team performance.
Tertiary qualification in Business, Information Technology, or a related discipline.

Desirable Criteria:


Experience within higher education, professional services, healthcare, or membership-based organisations.
Experience delivering or implementing enterprise platforms such as CRM, LMS, ERP, or digital portals.
Experience with Scaled Agile frameworks (SAFe or similar) in larger transformation programs.
Agile certifications such as Certified Scrum Master (CSM), SAFe, or ICP.
Project management certifications such as PMP or PRINCE2.
Experience managing technology vendors, implementation partners, and outsourced delivery teams.
Demonstrated ability to drive improvements in Agile maturity and delivery capability across teams

Please apply for a confidential chat.

Company description:



The Metcash story began from the humble origins of our first family-owned corner store in 1927. And for almost 100 years since then, we've been backing independents and strengthening the communities we operate in.

Today, Metcash (ASX: MTS) is Australia and New Zealand's leading independent retail partner and wholesaler in the food, liquor, hardware, and tools industries, with sales of $19.5 billion in FY25. We provide our network with marketing, purchasing, warehousing, distribution and systems support



Job description:



At Metcash, you're part of something bigger. We've got the heart of a small business with the strength and support of a big business.

When you join Metcash, your work makes a real difference to independent businesses and local communities, empowering you to:

Grow your career with tailored development programs
Thrive your way with flexible work options
Make your mark in a high-performing, values-led team

About the Opportunity

We have an exciting opportunity for a BI Reporting & Analytics Specialist to join our team based in Port Melbourne on a 12-month contract basis.

We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Data Analytics professional to join our innovative and dynamic team. As a BI Reporting & Analytics Specialist, you will play a crucial role in ensuring the integrity and security of our business systems data, building and maintaining our reports and analytics.

In this role, you will be at the forefront of leveraging big data technologies to build stunning dashboards, reports, and problem-solve complex business challenges. Your exceptional problem-solving skills and ability to translate complex data into clear insights will enable you to make a tangible impact on our business

In this Role

Unique opportunity to provide Data Engineering and BI Analytics to an established data rich business and an experienced team going through a growth period.
Leverage large-scale datasets to develop analytics, reports, and dashboards that support business growth and operational oversight.
Provide foundational systems, support and strategy across the business for all data repositories.
Creating and maintaining reporting in Cognos, Phocas and Power BI.
Strong problem-solving ability and practices to solve complex analytics tasks.
Provide foundational database support including data mapping and definition across the current SQL systems including BI and Cognos databases in support of the BI team.
Technical documentation, dictionaries and understanding the enterprise architecture around data flows across the core ERP and online systems.
Provide strategic guidance with data warehousing, data lakes and all repositories in support of our reporting, analytics and insights requirements.
Recommend and implement emerging reporting technologies.
Ability to question data, cleanse and map it to the stakeholders needs.
Responsible for the development of logical data models
Encourage the adoption of an organization's frameworks by providing documentation, training, sample code, and support
Provide consultation and contribute to project teams as necessary.

You're Likely a Match If

2.Enterprise and business architecture: Level 5 (SFIA)
Develop models and plans to drive the execution of the business strategy, taking advantage of opportunities to improve business performance. Contributes to creating and reviewing a systems capability strategy which meets the business's strategic requirements. Determines requirements and specifies effective business processes, through improvements in technology, information or data practices, organisation, roles, procedures and equipment.

3.Requirements definition and management: Level 6 (SFIA)
Champions the importance and value of requirements management principles and selecting effective requirements management life cycle models. Develops organisational policies, standards, and guidelines for requirements definition and management. Plans and leads scoping, requirements definition and priority setting for complex, strategic programmes. Drives adoption of, and adherence to, policies and standards. Develops new methods and organisational capabilities for requirements management.

4.Systems design: Level 6 (SFIA)
Develops and drives adoption of and adherence to organisational policies, standards, guidelines, and methods for system design. Champions the importance and value of system design principles and the selection of appropriate systems design life cycle models. Leads system design activities for strategic, large and complex systems development programmes. Develops effective implementation strategies consistent with specified requirements, architectures and constraints of performance and feasibility. Develop system design requiring the introduction of new technologies or new uses for existing technologies.

Desirable criteria
1.Methods and tools: Level 5 (SFIA)
Provides advice, guidance and expertise to promote adoption of methods and tools and adherence to policies and standards. Evaluates and selects appropriate methods and tools in line with agreed policies and standards. Contributes to organisational policies, standards, and guidelines for methods and tools. Implement methods and tools at programme, project and team levels including selection and tailoring in line with agreed standards. Manage reviews of the benefits and value of methods and tools. Identifies and recommends improvements.

2.Software design: Level 6 (SFIA)
Leads the selection and development of software design methods, tools and techniques. Develops organisational policies, standards, and guidelines for software design and software architecture. Ensures adherence to technical strategies and systems architectures (including security).

3.Specialist advice: Level 6 (SFIA)
Provides organisational leadership and guidelines to promote the development and exploitation of specialist knowledge in the organisation. Maintains a network of recognised experts (inside and/or outside the organisation) who can deliver expert advice in relevant areas. Provides input into professional development planning across a significant part of the organisation to further the development of appropriate expertise.

Job Title: Business Process Lead - Oracle EBS to Fusion Program
Ad Text:
The Business Process Lead will drive the end-to-end business process workstream for an enterprise transformation migrating from Oracle EBS to Oracle Fusion Cloud (ERP, HCM, EPM).

This role is accountable for leading process discovery, challenging and improving current ways of working, and designing a fit-to-standard future state aligned to Oracle Fusion capabilities and organizational strategy.

This is not a "process scribe" role.

We are looking for someone who is:

Strategic and pragmatic: improves outcomes without over-engineering.
Constructively challenging: questions legacy practices respectfully and effectively.
Systems thinking: understands upstream/downstream impacts across domains.
Outcome-driven: focuses on adoptable solutions and measurable benefits.
problem-solver: uses evidence, data, and clear decision frameworks
Essential requirements

Proven experience leading business process design for ERP transformations, ideally Oracle EBS to Oracle Fusion Cloud.
Strong understanding of Oracle ERP processes and capabilities (e.g., Financials, Procurement, Projects, Supply Chain—depending on scope).
Exposure to or working knowledge of Oracle Fusion HCM and/or EPM and how they integrate end-to-end with ERP.
Demonstrated ability to critically assess, simplify, and improve processes—balancing business needs with standard platform capabilities.
Strong facilitation skills: able to run workshops, manage conflict, and guide stakeholders to decisions.
Ability to create clear, structured artefacts (process maps, decision logs, requirements, fit-gap analysis, RACI).
Excellent communication skills—able to translate between business, functional consultants, and technical teams.
The role would suit a high level BA that's moved into working on Oracle EBS to Fusion transformation projects - or a funtcional architect that is business process driven.

Melbourne, Victoria | RACV Head Office

Exceptional communication, facilitation and stakeholder management skills.

 

There’s so much on offer when you join RACV with benefits including

Our flexible and informal work environment which helps bring balance between work and home.
